btx-precision.opportunity: BTX Precision scored 57 on opportunity with a perfect 100% fit, backed by two fresh government contract awards ($1.7M and $8.0M) and a demand spike on new program starts. However, risk is high at 94: the floor is near capacity, two quality escapes triggered customer source-inspection holds, and $9.3M in recompetes were lost. This is a strong target where new demand is real but delivery and quality reliability must be shored up to convert and protect the pipeline.

findings.sig-0001: A $1.7M government award adds funded, in-scope work that fits BTX's ITAR and precision-machining capabilities.
findings.sig-0002: An $8.0M government award is a major revenue driver and the largest opportunity input—prioritize resourcing to deliver it.
findings.sig-0003: A quality escape put a customer on source-inspection hold, signaling quality risk that can delay shipments and erode trust.
findings.sig-0004: Demand spiking on new program starts confirms growing need but also strains capacity.
findings.sig-0005: Extended lead-time quotes show the floor is near capacity, the top capacity-risk driver—new awards may outpace throughput.
findings.sig-0006: A second quality escape and source-inspection hold compounds the quality problem and adds competitive pressure.
findings.sig-0007: Losing a $6.0M long-held recompete signals real competitive pressure and lost recurring revenue.
findings.sig-0008: Losing a $3.3M long-held recompete further confirms erosion of incumbent business that must be defended.

anvil-manufacturing.opportunity: Anvil Manufacturing scores 83, driven by two multi-year production contract wins ($15.9M and $6.2M), a demand spike on new program starts, and a hiring surge — all signs of an existing customer scaling up fast. BTX has a 50% fit, able to serve their build-to-print and titanium needs, though NADCAP and sheet metal remain gaps. With new programs ramping and capacity likely stretched, this is a strong moment to expand our footprint on the work we can already deliver.
